title: Des applications pour parier sur le futur
gist: On prediction-market apps, people bet real money on future events like politics and sport — and they're booming, with even Meta building one. But they're controversial: some people bet using secret information, and others say it's really just gambling. The economist Al Roth, a Nobel Prize winner, argues that insider betting matters less here than on the stock market, worries most about powerful Washington insiders betting, and suggests apps let users cap their own losses.

body.fr: Sur les {{marchés de prédiction|prediction markets}}, les gens {{parient|bet (from parier)}} de l'argent.

bd.meaning: On prediction markets, people bet money.
chunks: on prediction markets (un marché = a market)
bd.key: The little words: de l' = 'some'. Before argent (money) French slips in de l' where English says nothing.

body.fr: Ils parient sur le {{futur|future}} : la {{politique|politics}}, le {{sport|sport}}.

bd.meaning: They bet on the future: politics, sport.
chunks: politics, sport — le / la still tag each noun
bd.key: le / la = 'the'. French keeps le or la even for general ideas: la politique, le sport.

bd.meaning: These apps are very popular today.
chunks: are very popular — populaires takes -s to match applications
bd.key: Adjective agrees with its noun. applications is plural, so populaires adds an -s to match.

body.fr: {{Meta veut|Meta wants}} faire une application comme ça aussi.

bd.meaning: Meta wants to make an app like that too.
chunks: to make an app — faire stays plain after veut
bd.key: Helper + plain verb. veut (wants) changes with the subject; the next verb faire stays plain.

chunks: there is / there are — a fixed phrase
bd.key: il y a = 'there is / are'. A set phrase — don't read it word for word as 'he has there'.

body.fr: Parfois, les gens parient avec des {{informations secrètes|secret information}}.

bd.meaning: Sometimes people bet with secret information.
chunks: secret information — secrètes comes AFTER the noun and takes -s
bd.key: Adjective after the noun, and agreeing. informations secrètes: the describing word follows the noun and adds -s for the plural.

body.fr: D'autres personnes disent que c'est seulement un {{jeu d'argent|gambling ('a money game')}}.

bd.meaning: Other people say it's just gambling.
chunks: other people say that (dire = to say)
bd.key: c'est = 'it is / that's'. The everyday set phrase for pointing at something: c'est seulement un jeu.

bd.meaning: An economist talks about this problem.
chunks: talks about (parler de = to talk about)
bd.key: parler de = 'to talk about'. Many French verbs pin on a small word like de — learn the pair together.

body.fr: Il s'appelle Al Roth et il a {{gagné|won (from gagner)}} un prix Nobel.

bd.meaning: His name is Al Roth and he won a Nobel Prize.
chunks: his name is Al Roth (literally 'he calls himself')
bd.key: Past = avoir + verb. a gagné = 'won': a (from avoir) + the past form gagné. Your 'past = avoir/être + verb' key.

body.fr: Pour lui, certains marchés {{choquent|shock}} les gens.

bd.meaning: For him, some markets shock people.
chunks: shock people — the -ent ending marks a plural subject
bd.key: -ent = a plural subject. certains marchés is plural, so the verb ends in silent -ent (it sounds the same as the singular).

body.fr: Dans la {{bourse|stock market}}, ce problème est très grave.

bd.meaning: On the stock market, this problem is very serious.
bd.key: Two ways to say 'is': c'est vs est. With a named subject (ce problème) use est; the bare 'it is' set phrase is c'est.

body.fr: Mais pour ces applications, Al Roth pense que c'est {{moins grave|less serious}}.

bd.meaning: But for these apps, Al Roth thinks it's less serious.
chunks: Al Roth thinks that (penser que = to think that)
bd.key: moins + adjective = 'less ___'. moins grave = 'less serious' — the easy way to compare with no special word.

body.fr: Al Roth a peur d'une autre chose.

bd.meaning: Al Roth is afraid of another thing.
chunks: Al Roth is afraid of (avoir peur de = to be afraid of)
another thing (de → d' before a vowel)
bd.key: avoir peur de = 'to be afraid of'. French says 'has fear of' — a peur de — using avoir where English uses 'to be'.

body.fr: Il n'aime pas quand les gens {{puissants|powerful}} de Washington parient.

bd.meaning: He doesn't like it when powerful people from Washington bet.
chunks: he doesn't like — the ne…pas sandwich around aime; ne → n' before a vowel
when the powerful people — puissants comes after the noun
bd.key: Negation sandwich: ne … pas. It wraps the verb: n'aime pas = 'does not like'.

body.fr: Al Roth a une idée pour {{aider|help}} les gens.

bd.meaning: Al Roth has an idea to help people.
chunks: to help / in order to help people
bd.key: pour + plain verb = 'in order to'. After pour the verb stays plain: aider, not conjugated.

body.fr: L'application peut {{fixer une limite|set a limit}}.

bd.meaning: The app can set a limit.
chunks: the app can (pouvoir = can / to be able to)
set a limit — fixer stays plain after peut
bd.key: Helper + plain verb, again. peut (can) carries the subject; fixer stays in its plain form.

body.fr: Par exemple : « Quand je {{perds|lose (from perdre)}} cent dollars, arrête. »

bd.meaning: For example: “When I lose a hundred dollars, stop.”
chunks: when I lose a hundred dollars (perdre = to lose)
bd.key: Giving an order: the bare verb. arrête = 'stop!' — a command drops the subject and just uses the verb form.

body.fr: C'est comme un {{barman|bartender}} : il arrête de servir une personne qui a {{trop bu|drunk too much}}.

bd.meaning: It's like a bartender: he stops serving a person who has drunk too much.
chunks: he stops serving (arrêter de + plain verb)
une personne qui a trop bu
a person who has drunk too much (a bu = past of boire)
bd.key: Past = avoir + verb. a trop bu = 'has drunk too much': a (avoir) + the past form bu (from boire).

grammar.fr: il a gagné un prix Nobel
grammar.en: avoir + past participle = the past (passé composé). a (from avoir) changes with the subject and the main verb takes its past form gagné. Your 'past = avoir/être + verb' key.

grammar.fr: Meta veut faire… / l'application peut fixer…
grammar.en: helper + plain verb. veut and peut carry the subject; the next verb (faire, fixer) stays plain. Your vouloir / pouvoir / devoir helpers all work this way.

grammar.fr: il n'aime pas / ce n'est pas grave
grammar.en: the ne … pas sandwich. It wraps the verb: n'aime pas. Before a vowel ne shrinks to n'.

quiz.q: Quelle est l'idée d'Al Roth pour aider les gens ?
quiz.a: L'application peut fixer une limite : elle s'arrête quand vous perdez trop d'argent. (The app can set a limit: it stops when you lose too much money.)

flag: marchés de prédiction is clear and understood, but many French writers use marchés prédictifs — I chose the more transparent form for you. This was a Q&A interview, compressed into a short neutral retelling: I dropped the DARPA 'terrorist market' example, the wheat-farmer futures analogy, and the fight over whether the C.F.T.C. or the states should regulate. certains marchés choquent les gens is my plain stand-in for Roth's idea of 'repugnant markets' — the gist, not a technical match. It's a neutral summary of your article, not my own view on betting.
